ops.title: Layover judgment: band-limited reordering, Judge phase, and layover preferences
ops.body:
# Layover-aware ranking: the deliberate decisions

Companion to the doc "Getaway skill redesign: balance-bias, seat quality ranking, transfer-first affordability" (98be9f3, commit 9a5fc1f6b1f2) — this layer composes with those decisions and reverts none. Each choice below won over a rejected alternative; do not undo one without understanding why.

## 1. Layover verdicts reorder only within a mileage band — unlike `barely`, which is an absolute tier

A `barely` seat is a categorical cabin failure, so it demotes across any price gap. Layovers are continuous and context-dependent, so a Judge verdict (`promote`/`neutral`/`demote`) shuffles options only inside a ~15% mileage band within each `(soft airline avoid, barely seat)` tier. The comparator stays `(soft, barely, mileage)`; the banded pass runs after it and before the `maxFinalists` cut. Bands are greedy (each opens at its first member's cost, extended with integer math `cost*100 <= bandStart*115` — float `50000*1.15` is 57499.999…, which would drop an exactly-15% candidate) and never chain. Both verdict directions act within the band — unlike seats, where suite-vs-solid promotion is a presentation-layer prose contract only. Rejected alternatives: an absolute layover tier (a demoted cheap option would sink below something 40% pricier, violating mileage dominance) and a judge-returned full ranking (would let a prompt override the deliberate comparator).

## 2. The ranking currency after expansion is `trip.mileageCost`, never the cached `row.mileage`

The cached teaser's cheapest routing can be rejected at expansion (avoid_transit, seats), leaving a pricier live trip. Judge input, band math, and the comparator's final term all read the bookable `trip.mileageCost`. Reverting any of them to `row.mileage` re-opens a confirmed defect: a stale 50k teaser banding beside a live 80k booking.

## 3. All-cabin 2× expand buffer

`expandTarget = min(ceil(maxFinalists * 2), 12)` in every cabin — generalizing (not reverting) the seat-quality layer's 1.5× business-only buffer. Layover data exists only in `/trips/{id}`, so re-ranking needs more expansions than finalists, and layovers matter in every cabin. The low-quota trim (`min(maxFinalists, 3, minQuota)`) still drops the buffer first.

## 4. Hybrids are never judged

A gateway stop is a chosen stopover, not a layover to score; hybrid ranking stays total-miles-then-cash, capped at maxHybrids. Hybrid award legs still carry structured segments + derived layovers for display.

## 5. Judge WebSearch is scoped to exit-worthiness; visas belong to Transit

The Judge may WebSearch only when a 6h+ layover's verdict turns on exit-worthiness it is unsure of (leave-and-return time, luggage storage). Formal transit-visa/entry flags are the Transit phase's job — the Judge never re-researches them. Duplicating visa research in the Judge was rejected: two agents answering the same question drift.

## 6. Resilience contracts

- Judge output must cover every judged candidate id exactly once (empty, partial, duplicated, or unknown-id responses take the logged fallback to pre-judge order — a dead judge never kills a run).
- An expansion returning `mileageCost` without `segments`/`layovers` arrays is malformed and drops like a dead agent (the `seatable` guard); TRIP_SCHEMA cannot require those fields because a null-`best` expansion legitimately returns only id+quota.
- The Judge phase is skipped entirely when every candidate is nonstop or only one survives.

## 7. Preference shape

`layovers: {style: minimize|explore, min_connection_minutes (positive integer — `. == floor` in prefs-set, fractional values would abort plan-trip), prefer_cities, avoid_cities}`. prefs-set requires the object sent whole (all four fields, like balances/statuses/documents); an absent key means shipped defaults, so pre-existing files validate. City lists are shape-only at prefs-set (IATA enforcement lives at the save layer, deliberately matching sibling `avoid_transit`). Workflow args are camelCase (`floorMinutes`, `preferCities`) per the existing snake→camel mapping.

## Verified API semantics the derivation rests on

`DepartsAt`/`ArrivesAt` are each airport's local wall-clock with a spurious trailing `Z` — same-airport (layover) diffs via `fromdateiso8601` are exact; cross-airport diffs are wrong (use `Duration`/`TotalDuration`, which are minutes). Verified live 2026-07-13 (trip 39rPl0klqPT3Mf35bExkNAl2hIE: CLT 255 min, JFK 212 min, TotalDuration reconciles exactly).
ops.when: Before modifying route ranking, the Judge phase, the banded reorder, layover preferences, or the expand buffer in the getaway skill.
